target_emits.name: create_captive_portal
target_emits.purpose: Create the captive-portal profile in the Library. The preprocessing normalizer flattens the AOS 8 cp_auth_profile (one-level-wrapped scalars, empty-object presence flags, the inverted protocol-http -> use-https, the auth-protocol enum, and black/white-list arrays) into a common _<param> shape; key_mappings below map that onto the Central body per the HPE LLD captive-portal mapping. Optional fields the operator never set are dropped so Central applies its own defaults.
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST per source cp_auth_profile record.

target_emits.name: assign_captive_portal
target_emits.purpose: Assign the captive-portal profile to the target scope so the gateway aaa-profile / wlan-ssid can reference it. Multi-device-function array packed into a single POST.

config-assignment: {one entry per device_function in target_meta.device_functions: { scope-id, device-function, profile-type='captive-portal', profile-instance='{name}' }}

target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST; all device-functions share the array.

target_meta.device_function_filtering_rule: Captive-portal profiles back gateway-terminated guest WLAN auth; the AOS 8 -> Central migration targets MOBILITY_GW. Operators with AP-direct (bridge) captive portal can override device_functions in runtime_values.

target_scope_id_resolution.rule: Central scope_id is supplied by the consuming skill at runtime, derived from the AOS 8 cp_auth_profile record's binding scope.
target_scope_id_resolution.input: Source binding scope (string path / identifier); skill-specific

dependencies.depends_on: central:server_group (cp_server_group -> server-group reference) and central:role (default-role / default-guest-role references). Those referenced profiles should exist in Central before / alongside this captive-portal POST.

depended_by.relation: the gateway aaa-profile (and the wlan-ssid CaptivePortalConfig) reference a captive-portal profile by name
depended_by.notes: AOS 8 aaa_prof.cp_auth_profile names a cp_auth_profile; the Central aaa-profile / wlan-ssid references the captive-portal profile by name. Run captive-portal BEFORE the aaa-profile / wlan-ssid translation.

objects.notes: REST schema 'cp_auth_profile' (CLI noun: aaa authentication captive-portal). Mixes one-level-wrapped scalars (cp_redirect_url:{redirect-url}), empty-object presence flags (apple_cna_bypass:{}=enabled), and black/white-list arrays ([{black-list:<name>}] / [{white-list:<name>}]). authentication_method.captive_auth_t enum is ['PAP','MSCHAPv2','chap']. The normalizer flattens these per the HPE LLD captive-portal mapping.

name.to: Central captive-portal 'name' (path param step 1, profile-instance step 2, body 'name')

default_guest_role.notes: LLD: default-guest-role <role>. References a Central role (central:role).

default_role.notes: LLD: default-role <role>. References a Central role.

server_group.notes: LLD: server-group <group-name>. References a Central server-group (central:server_group).

user_logon.notes: LLD flag: user-logon (default True; AOS 8 allow_user).

use_https.notes: LLD INVERTED: AOS 8 protocol-http (present = plain HTTP) -> use-https=False. Normalizer sets False only when protocol-http is operator-set; omitted otherwise -> Central default use-https=True.

auth_protocol.notes: LLD enum_map: authentication_method.captive_auth_t (['PAP','MSCHAPv2','chap']) -> Central auth-protocol (PAP/MSCHAPv2/CHAP). Default PAP.

unmapped_fields.reason: Per the HPE LLD: proxy has no CNX captive-portal equivalent; login-page exists in YANG but is hidden (ac-ext:hidden 'all') and not exposed in the CNX API; clone is an operational command, not a config property; _flags are AOS 8-internal metadata (consumer filters inherited/default records).

ignored_variants.reason: AOS 8 ships a built-in default captive-portal profile. Consumer filters _flags.default / _flags.system / _flags.inherited (at definition scope) BEFORE calling emit_calls, matching the central:auth_server / server_group / net_group / role convention.

draft_notes: v1 translation, Gateway-targeted. POST /captive-portal/{name} then config-assignment to MOBILITY_GW. Field/param correspondence + transforms + defaults taken field-by-field from the HPE LLD captive-portal mapping (aaa authentication captive-portal -> /captive-portal/{name}).

TEMPLATE COMPLIANCE: standard Paradigm-B (per-field key_mappings + structurally complete body). The presence-flag / inverted-flag / enum / array quirks are flattened by the normalizer (translations/preprocessing/aos8_captive_portal.py); the JSON stays thin and reviewable.

INVERTED FLAG: AOS 8 protocol-http <-> Central use-https are inverted. The normalizer emits use-https=False only when protocol-http is operator-set; otherwise it's omitted so Central's default (use-https=True) applies.

DEPENDENCY: references central:role (default-role / default-guest-role) and central:server_group (server-group). Run those first. In turn the gateway aaa-profile / wlan-ssid reference this captive-portal by name (aaa_prof.cp_auth_profile) -- run captive-portal before those.

PRESENCE-FLAG EDGE CASE: AOS 8 default-True flags (user-logon, enable-welcome-page, logout-popup-window) are emitted True when operator-set and omitted otherwise (-> Central default True). An explicit operator DISABLE of a default-True flag is not distinguishable from 'left default' in the empty-object REST shape; flag as a caveat for those three if a disabled sample surfaces.

NEEDS-PREVIEW: the config-assignment profile-type ('captive-portal') and the exact Central body field names (taken from the LLD, not yet all live-verified against a configured captive-portal) -- confirm via central_translation_preview against a tenant with captive-portal profiles configured.
Idempotency: SHARED POST for an existing captive-portal name returns HTTP 409; consumer handles 409 idempotently.
